steps.action: It was created from the Gaelic folk dance repert with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.
steps.text: It was created from the Gaelic folk dance repert with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.

steps.action: Highland dancing is often performed with the acc
steps.text: Highland dancing is often performed with the accompaniment of Highland bagpipe music, and dancers wear specialised shoes called ghillies or pumps.

steps.action: Highland dance should not be confused with Scott
steps.text: Highland dance should not be confused with Scottish country dance, cèilidh dancing, or clog dancing, although they too may be performed at Highland games and like competitions.

steps.action: In Highland dancing, the dancers dance on the ba
steps.text: In Highland dancing, the dancers dance on the balls of the feet.

steps.action: Some Highland dances do derive from traditional
steps.text: Some Highland dances do derive from traditional social dances.

steps.action: Even so, in competitions, the Highland reel danc
steps.text: Even so, in competitions, the Highland reel dancers are judged individually.

steps.action: All but three Highland dances are danced solo an
steps.text: All but three Highland dances are danced solo and those three dances are variations of each other.

steps.action: While some dance studios teach both, they are tw
steps.text: While some dance studios teach both, they are two distinct styles, not just in the attire.

steps.action: There is a greater use of choreography than trad
steps.text: There is a greater use of choreography than traditional movements.

steps.action: It was "created from the Gaelic folk dance reper
steps.text: It was "created from the Gaelic folk dance repertoire, but formalised with the conventions of ballet".

steps.action: It seems that forms of sword dancing were perfor
steps.text: It seems that forms of sword dancing were performed by warriors in many parts of Europe in the prehistoric period.

steps.action: Forms of sword dancing are also attested in the
steps.text: Forms of sword dancing are also attested in the late Medieval period.

steps.action: Ritualistic and combative dances that imitated e
steps.text: Ritualistic and combative dances that imitated epic deeds and martial skills were a familiar feature in Scottish tradition and folklore.

steps.action: The earliest reference to these dances in Scotla
steps.text: The earliest reference to these dances in Scotland is mentioned in the Scotichronicon which was compiled in Scotland by Walter Bower in the 1440s.

steps.action: At the head of this procession were the skilled
steps.text: At the head of this procession were the skilled musicians with many sorts of pipe music including the music of bagpipes, and behind them others splendidly performing a war-dance with intricate weaving in and out.

steps.action: Bringing up the rear was a figure regarding whom
steps.text: Bringing up the rear was a figure regarding whom it was difficult to decide whether it was a man or an apparition.

steps.action: When it looked as if he was disappearing from ev
steps.text: When it looked as if he was disappearing from everyone's sight, the whole frenzied procession halted, the song died away, the music faded, and the dancing contingent froze suddenly and unexpectedly.

steps.action: In 1573, Scottish mercenaries are said to have p
steps.text: In 1573, Scottish mercenaries are said to have performed a Scottish sword dance before the Swedish King, John III, at a banquet held in Stockholm Castle.

steps.action: The dance, 'a natural feature of the festivities
steps.text: The dance, 'a natural feature of the festivities', was used as part of a plot to assassinate the King, where the conspirators were able to bare their weapons without arising suspicion.

steps.action: Highland dancing was an integral part of the Gam
steps.text: Highland dancing was an integral part of the Games from the very start of their modern revival, but the selection of dances performed at Games was intentionally narrowed down, mostly for the convenience of judges.

steps.action: The nature of these displays and competitions al
steps.text: The nature of these displays and competitions also affected the style of the dancing itself.

steps.action: Judges of competitions were local persons, witho
steps.text: Judges of competitions were local persons, without specific standards for attire or the steps to the danced.

steps.action: Local Caledonian societies trained young dancers
steps.text: Local Caledonian societies trained young dancers in the way of each society.

steps.action: Slowly consistency of steps was achieved, and da
steps.text: Slowly consistency of steps was achieved, and dancing-specific organisations were established.
